channel.title: Colors of Provence — A Traveler's Companion
channel.description: Short spoken-word essays on the history, politics, culture, and food of Paris, Provence, and the Rhône — a personal companion for the journey from Arles to Lyon.
channel.itunes:author: France Podcast
channel.itunes:summary: Short spoken-word essays on the history, politics, culture, and food of Paris, Provence, and the Rhône — a personal companion for the journey from Arles to Lyon.

image.title: Colors of Provence — A Traveler's Companion

item.title: The Café and the Two Revolutions
item.itunes:title: The Café and the Two Revolutions
item.description:
You have been walking Saint-Germain-des-Prés, so let me send you down one particular street, the rue de l'Ancienne Comédie, a narrow lane just off the Boulevard Saint-Germain.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0200-cafe-two-revolutions.html
item.itunes:summary:
You have been walking Saint-Germain-des-Prés, so let me send you down one particular street, the rue de l'Ancienne Comédie, a narrow lane just off the Boulevard Saint-Germain.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0200-cafe-two-revolutions.html

item.title: The Restaurant Was Born Here — Paris on a Plate
item.itunes:title: The Restaurant Was Born Here — Paris on a Plate
item.description:
Let me start with a claim that sounds like tourist-board puffery but happens to be more or less true: the restaurant, as an institution, was invented in Paris. Not cooking, obviously, and not eating out, which is as old as cities.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0250-parisian-food.html
item.itunes:summary:
Let me start with a claim that sounds like tourist-board puffery but happens to be more or less true: the restaurant, as an institution, was invented in Paris. Not cooking, obviously, and not eating out, which is as old as cities.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0250-parisian-food.html

item.title: Julia Child's Paris — Sole Meunière and the Making of an Appetite
item.itunes:title: Julia Child's Paris — Sole Meunière and the Making of an Appetite
item.description:
There is a meal that a lot of Americans of a certain age can describe as if they were there, even though only one person actually was. It is November of nineteen forty-eight.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0275-julia-child.html
item.itunes:summary:
There is a meal that a lot of Americans of a certain age can describe as if they were there, even though only one person actually was. It is November of nineteen forty-eight.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0275-julia-child.html

item.title: The Guillotine and the Problem of Legitimacy
item.itunes:title: The Guillotine and the Problem of Legitimacy
item.description:
Stand for a moment in the middle of the Place de la Concorde. It is the largest public square in Paris, and probably the most trafficked open space you will cross on your entire walk. Cars stream around it in a great impatient orbit. Two fountains throw water into the light.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0300-guillotine-legitimacy.html
item.itunes:summary:
Stand for a moment in the middle of the Place de la Concorde. It is the largest public square in Paris, and probably the most trafficked open space you will cross on your entire walk. Cars stream around it in a great impatient orbit. Two fountains throw water into the light.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0300-guillotine-legitimacy.html

item.title: The Glow in the Garden — Marie Curie's Paris
item.itunes:title: The Glow in the Garden — Marie Curie's Paris
item.description:
She is one of the most extraordinary people ever to walk the streets you have been walking, and her story starts a short distance from where you are. If you go a little east from Saint-Germain-des-Prés, up into the Latin Quarter around the Sorbonne, you come to a street that is actually named rue Pierre et Marie…
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0350-marie-curie.html
item.itunes:summary:
She is one of the most extraordinary people ever to walk the streets you have been walking, and her story starts a short distance from where you are. If you go a little east from Saint-Germain-des-Prés, up into the Latin Quarter around the Sorbonne, you come to a street that is actually named rue Pierre et Marie…
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0350-marie-curie.html

item.title: What the Revolution Actually Changed
item.itunes:title: What the Revolution Actually Changed
item.description:
When we say "the French Revolution," a certain picture arrives almost automatically. A crowd. A blade. A king kneeling on a scaffold in what is now the Place de la Concorde, a few minutes' walk from wherever you are standing right now.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0400-revolution-changed.html
item.itunes:summary:
When we say "the French Revolution," a certain picture arrives almost automatically. A crowd. A blade. A king kneeling on a scaffold in what is now the Place de la Concorde, a few minutes' walk from wherever you are standing right now.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0400-revolution-changed.html

item.title: Laïcité — Why France Does Secularism Differently
item.itunes:title: Laïcité — Why France Does Secularism Differently
item.description:
You are walking through Paris, and sooner or later you will pass a church that the state owns. Notre-Dame, Saint-Sulpice, the Madeleine — these great stone buildings belong to the French government, not to the Catholic Church, and the priests who serve in them do so as guests in a public building.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0500-laicite.html
item.itunes:summary:
You are walking through Paris, and sooner or later you will pass a church that the state owns. Notre-Dame, Saint-Sulpice, the Madeleine — these great stone buildings belong to the French government, not to the Catholic Church, and the priests who serve in them do so as guests in a public building.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0500-laicite.html

item.title: The Painted Promise
item.itunes:title: The Painted Promise
item.description:
Before you board, look up. You are in the Gare de Lyon, in the great hall where the departure boards flicker and everyone stands with their heads tilted back, waiting for a platform number to appear. The French have an old name for a room like this.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0550-gare-de-lyon-fresco.html
item.itunes:summary:
Before you board, look up. You are in the Gare de Lyon, in the great hall where the departure boards flicker and everyone stands with their heads tilted back, waiting for a platform number to appear. The French have an old name for a room like this.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0550-gare-de-lyon-fresco.html

item.title: The Arrow from Paris — The TGV and the Shape of France
item.itunes:title: The Arrow from Paris — The TGV and the Shape of France
item.description:
You are standing under the great vaulted hall of the Gare de Lyon in Paris, and if you have a few minutes before you board, look up. There is a restaurant here called Le Train Bleu, all gilded plaster and painted ceilings, named for the old luxury sleeper that carried the wealthy down to the Riviera.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0600-tgv.html
item.itunes:summary:
You are standing under the great vaulted hall of the Gare de Lyon in Paris, and if you have a few minutes before you board, look up. There is a restaurant here called Le Train Bleu, all gilded plaster and painted ceilings, named for the old luxury sleeper that carried the wealthy down to the Riviera.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0600-tgv.html

item.title: The Cure and the Light
item.itunes:title: The Cure and the Light
item.description:
As the train runs south and the light through the window begins to change, sharpen, get harder and cleaner and more golden, it is worth asking a simple question. Who else came this way, and why? Because you are not the first person to travel south in France looking for something.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0625-who-came-south.html
item.itunes:summary:
As the train runs south and the light through the window begins to change, sharpen, get harder and cleaner and more golden, it is worth asking a simple question. Who else came this way, and why? Because you are not the first person to travel south in France looking for something.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0625-who-came-south.html

item.title: The Midi — How the South Became France
item.itunes:title: The Midi — How the South Became France
item.description:
Somewhere in the second half of this train ride, without any announcement, you will cross a border. There is no sign for it, no passport check, no line drawn on the map, but it is as real as any frontier France has ever had.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0650-the-midi.html
item.itunes:summary:
Somewhere in the second half of this train ride, without any announcement, you will cross a border. There is no sign for it, no passport check, no line drawn on the map, but it is as real as any frontier France has ever had.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0650-the-midi.html

item.title: The Tongues of the South
item.itunes:title: The Tongues of the South
item.description:
Somewhere on this journey you are going to notice that the map is lying to you a little. It will tell you that you are in France, and you are, but the words painted on the signs at the edge of the villages will sometimes come in pairs.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0675-tongues-of-the-south.html
item.itunes:summary:
Somewhere on this journey you are going to notice that the map is lying to you a little. It will tell you that you are in France, and you are, but the words painted on the signs at the edge of the villages will sometimes come in pairs.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0675-tongues-of-the-south.html

item.title: Arles as Rome
item.itunes:title: Arles as Rome
item.description:
Stand at the edge of the Arles amphitheater, the Arènes, and let the size of it settle on you before you say anything clever about it. Two tiers of arches, more than a hundred of them, a great stone oval built in the last years of the first century, around ninety in the common era, when Rome was near the height of its…
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0700-arles-as-rome.html
item.itunes:summary:
Stand at the edge of the Arles amphitheater, the Arènes, and let the size of it settle on you before you say anything clever about it. Two tiers of arches, more than a hundred of them, a great stone oval built in the last years of the first century, around ninety in the common era, when Rome was near the height of its…
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0700-arles-as-rome.html

item.title: Arles on Foot
item.itunes:title: Arles on Foot
item.description:
This one is a walk. Put your comfortable shoes on, because we are going to make a loop through the old town of Arles, six stops, maybe a mile and a half all told, and the whole point is to do it on foot, slowly, letting the city change under your feet. Arles is small.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0750-arles-on-foot.html
item.itunes:summary:
This one is a walk. Put your comfortable shoes on, because we are going to make a loop through the old town of Arles, six stops, maybe a mile and a half all told, and the whole point is to do it on foot, slowly, letting the city change under your feet. Arles is small.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0750-arles-on-foot.html

item.title: Van Gogh's Yellow
item.itunes:title: Van Gogh's Yellow
item.description:
You have come to Arles for a lot of reasons, but let us be honest about the one under all the others. You came for the yellow. And the remarkable thing, standing here, is that the yellow is real. It is not something Vincent van Gogh invented in his head and imposed on this town. It is in the light.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0800-van-gogh-yellow.html
item.itunes:summary:
You have come to Arles for a lot of reasons, but let us be honest about the one under all the others. You came for the yellow. And the remarkable thing, standing here, is that the yellow is real. It is not something Vincent van Gogh invented in his head and imposed on this town. It is in the light.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0800-van-gogh-yellow.html

item.title: Les Alyscamps and the Bones of the Dead
item.itunes:title: Les Alyscamps and the Bones of the Dead
item.description:
Walk out past the edge of the old town of Arles, away from the amphitheater and the cafés, and you come to a place that feels like it belongs to a different order of time.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0900-alyscamps.html
item.itunes:summary:
Walk out past the edge of the old town of Arles, away from the amphitheater and the cafés, and you come to a place that feels like it belongs to a different order of time.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/0900-alyscamps.html

item.title: The City of Popes
item.itunes:title: The City of Popes
item.description:
Stand in front of the Palais des Papes and let your eye climb the walls, and the first thing to tell you is that this is the wrong shape for a church. There is no soaring facade begging you to look up toward heaven, no rose window, no welcoming porch.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1000-city-of-popes.html
item.itunes:summary:
Stand in front of the Palais des Papes and let your eye climb the walls, and the first thing to tell you is that this is the wrong shape for a church. There is no soaring facade begging you to look up toward heaven, no rose window, no welcoming porch.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1000-city-of-popes.html

item.title: Pont du Gard and the Genius of Roman Water
item.itunes:title: Pont du Gard and the Genius of Roman Water
item.description:
Stand at the foot of the Pont du Gard, on the bank of the Gardon where the river cuts its gorge, and look up before you reach for any word to describe it. Three tiers of arches, stacked one on top of another, in a stone the color of honey, climbing to roughly forty-nine meters above the water.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1100-pont-du-gard.html
item.itunes:summary:
Stand at the foot of the Pont du Gard, on the bank of the Gardon where the river cuts its gorge, and look up before you reach for any word to describe it. Three tiers of arches, stacked one on top of another, in a stone the color of honey, climbing to roughly forty-nine meters above the water.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1100-pont-du-gard.html

item.title: The Rhône as a Highway
item.itunes:title: The Rhône as a Highway
item.description:
Stand at the rail for a moment and just watch the water go by. If you look closely, you will see that the river is not idling. The Rhône moves. Even here, on this broad calm reach, there is a muscle to it, a steady southward pull, and that pull is the whole story of this valley.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1200-rhone-highway.html
item.itunes:summary:
Stand at the rail for a moment and just watch the water go by. If you look closely, you will see that the river is not idling. The Rhône moves. Even here, on this broad calm reach, there is a muscle to it, a steady southward pull, and that pull is the whole story of this valley.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1200-rhone-highway.html

item.title: The Truffle and the Terroir
item.itunes:title: The Truffle and the Terroir
item.description:
Picture the thing you are about to go looking for. It is dark, roughly the size of a walnut or a small plum, its surface pebbled like the skin of some tiny reptile. Cut it open and the inside is nearly black, marbled with fine white veins, like a slice of polished stone.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1300-truffle-terroir.html
item.itunes:summary:
Picture the thing you are about to go looking for. It is dark, roughly the size of a walnut or a small plum, its surface pebbled like the skin of some tiny reptile. Cut it open and the inside is nearly black, marbled with fine white veins, like a slice of polished stone.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1300-truffle-terroir.html

item.title: Côtes du Rhône — A River You Can Drink
item.itunes:title: Côtes du Rhône — A River You Can Drink
item.description:
Stand on the deck this morning and look across the river. There, on the far bank at Tain-l'Hermitage, directly opposite our mooring at Tournon, is a single steep hill, maybe six hundred feet of it, rising off the valley floor like the back of a whale, and it is combed top to bottom with terraced vineyards, little…
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1400-cotes-du-rhone.html
item.itunes:summary:
Stand on the deck this morning and look across the river. There, on the far bank at Tain-l'Hermitage, directly opposite our mooring at Tournon, is a single steep hill, maybe six hundred feet of it, rising off the valley floor like the back of a whale, and it is combed top to bottom with terraced vineyards, little…
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1400-cotes-du-rhone.html

item.title: Vienne, Rome, and Jazz
item.itunes:title: Vienne, Rome, and Jazz
item.description:
Walk into the center of Vienne and you will come, almost by accident, upon a Roman temple standing in the middle of a town square as if it had simply never left. This is the Temple of Augustus and Livia, and I want you to understand how strange and wonderful that is before we say anything else.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1500-vienne-rome-jazz.html
item.itunes:summary:
Walk into the center of Vienne and you will come, almost by accident, upon a Roman temple standing in the middle of a town square as if it had simply never left. This is the Temple of Augustus and Livia, and I want you to understand how strange and wonderful that is before we say anything else.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1500-vienne-rome-jazz.html

item.title: The Capital of Eating
item.itunes:title: The Capital of Eating
item.description:
Let's begin where you'll almost certainly end up your first evening in Lyon: at a small table, in a narrow room, in a place called a bouchon. There's paper on the table, maybe, and the walls are crowded with old photographs and enamel signs. A carafe of Beaujolais lands in front of you without much ceremony.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1600-capital-of-eating.html
item.itunes:summary:
Let's begin where you'll almost certainly end up your first evening in Lyon: at a small table, in a narrow room, in a place called a bouchon. There's paper on the table, maybe, and the walls are crowded with old photographs and enamel signs. A carafe of Beaujolais lands in front of you without much ceremony.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1600-capital-of-eating.html

item.title: Silk, Revolt, and the Canuts
item.itunes:title: Silk, Revolt, and the Canuts
item.description:
Look up. That is where this story starts, on the hill above the two rivers, the one Lyon calls the Croix-Rousse. You will notice the windows first. They are enormous, absurdly tall for ordinary houses, reaching nearly floor to ceiling, and they are everywhere on this hill.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1700-canuts.html
item.itunes:summary:
Look up. That is where this story starts, on the hill above the two rivers, the one Lyon calls the Croix-Rousse. You will notice the windows first. They are enormous, absurdly tall for ordinary houses, reaching nearly floor to ceiling, and they are everywhere on this hill.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1700-canuts.html

item.title: Fourvière and the Two Hills
item.itunes:title: Fourvière and the Two Hills
item.description:
Stand up at the Basilica of Notre-Dame de Fourvière, on the western hill, with your back to all that ornate stone, and look out over Lyon. The two rivers, the Saône and the Rhône, coming together below you.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1800-fourviere-two-hills.html
item.itunes:summary:
Stand up at the Basilica of Notre-Dame de Fourvière, on the western hill, with your back to all that ornate stone, and look out over Lyon. The two rivers, the Saône and the Rhône, coming together below you.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1800-fourviere-two-hills.html

item.title: Olive Oil and Butter — France's Three Kitchens
item.itunes:title: Olive Oil and Butter — France's Three Kitchens
item.description:
There is a line that runs across France that you will never see on any map, and yet it may be the truest border the country has. It is not a line of language or of politics. It is a line of fat. South of it, people cook in olive oil.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1900-regional-cooking.html
item.itunes:summary:
There is a line that runs across France that you will never see on any map, and yet it may be the truest border the country has. It is not a line of language or of politics. It is a line of fat. South of it, people cook in olive oil.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1900-regional-cooking.html

item.title: How to Drink France — A Personal Wine Companion
item.itunes:title: How to Drink France — A Personal Wine Companion
item.description:
There is one small thing that trips up nearly every American in front of a French wine list, and once you see it, the whole country opens up like a door. Back home, a label tells you the grape. You buy a Cabernet, a Pinot Noir, a Chardonnay; the grape is the brand, the star, the name on the marquee.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1950-wine-companion.html
item.itunes:summary:
There is one small thing that trips up nearly every American in front of a French wine list, and once you see it, the whole country opens up like a door. Back home, a label tells you the grape. You buy a Cabernet, a Pinot Noir, a Chardonnay; the grape is the brand, the star, the name on the marquee.
Full transcript: https://ardell.github.io/france_podcast/episodes/1950-wine-companion.html
